title: For Oman, the Strait of Hormuz is a windfall and a burden
subhead: The monarchy, which draws its influence from its geographic position on the edge of the maritime corridor, has been unsettled by Iran's strategy of monetizing the passage. With its hands tied by a longstanding tradition of mediation and neutrality, Muscat has struggled to assert itself.

title: After a foiled bombing outside Bank of America in Paris, five arrests and questions over Iran's role
Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
Trump threatens to destroy Kharg Island, Iran's oil export hub
subhead: The US president said he would destroy the island, which houses oil wells, power plants, and other civilian infrastructure, if a deal is not soon reached to end the war.

title: G7 ministers call for stabilizing energy markets amid Middle East war
subhead: Ministers of the group of industrial powers said they are prepared to take 'all necessary measures' to stabilize the global energy market, which is facing disruptions with Middle Eastern oil shipments.

title: Celine Dion announces return to Paris stage this September and October
subhead: The 58-year-old Canadian star had been on a long hiatus after a health condition, but announced on Monday that she was ready to return to the French capital for 10 shows.

title: Assault, attempted murder, assassination: The rogue Freemasons going on trial in Paris
subhead: A coach, a race car driver, a trade unionist: Nothing seemed to connect these otherwise unremarkable individuals. Yet all were targeted by the same criminal organization. At its head were prominent members of a Masonic lodge in a Paris suburb. These 'brothers,' now enemies, are standing trial from March 30.

The American dream crumbles for French students
subhead: Since Donald Trump's return to the White House, several institutions have reported a decline in applications to US campuses. Amid political tensions, stricter visa scrutiny and a shift favoring colleges in Asia, a subtle downturn is taking shape.

title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
subhead: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.

title: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

title: Iranians are caught in the trap of Trump's incoherence
'Trump's vociferations no longer reassure financial markets'
'As he approached the sands of the Arabian-Persian Gulf, Trump fell prey to a common phenomenon in these lands: mirages'
